section:
**READING TEST**
In the Reading test, you will read a variety of texts and answer several different types of reading comprehension questions. The entire Reading test will last 75 minutes. There are three parts, and directions are given for each part. You are encouraged to answer as many questions as possible within the time allowed.
You must mark your answers on the separate answer sheet. Do not write your answers in your test book.

## PART 5
**Directions:** A word or phrase is missing in each of the sentences below. Four answer choices are given below each sentence. Select the best answer to complete the sentence. Then mark the letter (A), (B), (C), or (D) on your answer sheet.
stem: Aberdeen Bank offers a range of financial services ------ the needs of its customers.
------ staff are asked to provide a backup cell phone number and e-mail address.
Today, Mr. Rahn will present ------ ideas to improve the company's accounting software.
The firm's one-hour lunch policy is ------ enforced, so do not return late.
Ms. Martinova's promotion to chief financial officer is contingent ------ approval by our board of executives.
The peninsula's southernmost portion is rarely visited because it is not ------ accessible to travelers.
The Vaknis Group ------ all clients a flat consultation fee, to be paid in advance.
On August 19, employees will not be able to access their e-mail account ------ time sheet.
Walk-in clients are ------ a reliable source of business for the Auburn Hair Salon.
By the end of the year, all packaging used for Schaefer's food products will be ------ recyclable.
Highbrook Hotel staff are trained to provide each guest with an exceptional ------.
In ------ for their help, volunteers at the library's book sale were given personalized tea mugs.
Because employees must learn to use the new software, several training sessions will take place ------ the next two weeks.
Flight attendants asked passengers to take their seats ------ upon boarding the plane.
Last July, Rojas Rieper LLC ------ a grand opening celebration.
Mr. Kim was one ------ three people who received the firm's Competitive Edge award.
Interviews begin today for the production manager ------ at Zhu Pharmaceuticals.
Tours of the historic courthouse are offered twice a week ------ the summer.
------ the next few months, Abundi Ltd. will open its fourth pharmaceutical laboratory in New Zealand.
The ------ at Yohanan Company organizes the delivery of supplies to all conference locations.
The owners of Rowecroft Porcelain ------ to begin production of casual dinnerware sets next month.
Mykos Auto Makers agreed to grant long-term factory employees ------ vacation time.
The customer ------ believed that the coat he had purchased was waterproof.
All cars built by Roadway Motors come ------ with an alarm reminding drivers to buckle their seat belts.
The components of the CT640 dishwasher are largely the same as ------ of earlier models.
The Nakato Group has won several industry awards for its innovative marketing ------.
Krit Pinthong's new mystery novel is the most widely ------ book of the year.
The leadership team was quite relieved ------ the Toyvar building project was completed under budget.
------ two floors of offices, the building offers several retail spaces.
------ regarding construction noise and traffic delays should be directed to Mr. Jasdi, the project manager.
section:
## PART 6
**Directions:** Read the texts that follow. A word, phrase, or sentence is missing in parts of each text. Four answer choices for each question are given below the text. Select the best answer to complete the text. Then mark the letter (A), (B), (C), or (D) on your answer sheet.
stimulus:
**Questions 131-134** refer to the following advertisement.
> **JOIN THE RGBS AUTOMOTIVE TEAM** > > RGBS Automotive is ------ hiring full-time and part-time workers. Apply today! ------ could become part of our manufacturing team! We make high-tech products ------ found in cars and trucks of all kinds. > > RGBS Automotive pays well, and we provide ongoing training plus opportunities for promotion. > > ------ . Go to www.rgbsautomotive.com for more details and to fill out an application.
option_text: Ours is a highly competitive industry.
RGBS Automotive began doing business 45 years ago.
We also offer a generous number of vacation days.
RGBS Automotive sells a wide selection of merchandise.
stimulus:
**Questions 135-138** refer to the following memo.
> **MEMO** > > To: Marketing Team > From: Alyssa Jacobs, Project Manager > Date: 27 September > Subject: Meeting wrap-up > > Thank you again to all of you for your fine work on the upcoming marketing campaign for the Turbo Omega 2 smartphone. As ------ during yesterday's meeting, our goal is to launch the television, radio, and social media advertisements on 1 November. The phone itself will be in stores by 1 December. ------ . > > ------ , Gary Carollo will draft the press release and post it on our shared drive, where all of you will be able to read it. Please e-mail any ------ for changes to Gary within one week. He will present his final draft at our next regular meeting. We will also tie up any loose ends at that time.
option_text: Remember to turn off your phone at the end of the workday.
This gives us one month to generate consumer excitement.
The wholesale price can also be adjusted if necessary.
These new features will surely increase Turbo Omega 2 sales.
stimulus:
**Questions 139-142** refer to the following product information.
> **Handmade Silk Blouse by Coreopsis Textiles, Size Medium, £45** > > Coreopsis Textiles' silk blouses are created from vintage fabrics and other recycled components that we sew together in a patchwork fashion. Because all 139. ______ garments are handcrafted, each is one of a kind. 140. ______. Unique variations within each piece are part of the charm of Coreopsis Textiles' products. The blouse you receive will be similar in style but not identical to the one pictured. > > Please note that this garment is 141. ______. It is recommended that you either hand-wash it or wash it in cold water on the gentle cycle in a mesh bag. While the item has been prewashed, dryer heat may cause it to shrink. 142. ______, it is important that it be hung to air dry.
option_text: This is a hassle-free return policy.
stimulus:
**Questions 143-146** refer to the following e-mail.
> To: Shu Jiang > From: Maxwell Baschet > Date: 4 April > Subject: Your contract > Attachment: Jiang contract > > Dear Ms. Jiang, > > We are pleased you 143. ______ Maple Road Storage for your storage needs. I have attached a copy of your signed contract. 144. ______. > > Do you know about our referral program? You will receive one free month of storage if someone you recommend signs a contract with us. The new tenant will receive a free month 145. ______. You can find more information about this program and all our special 146. ______ on our Web site at www.mapleroadstorage.ca. > > Sincerely, > > Maxwell Baschet, Site Manager
option_text: If your contact information changes, please let us know.
We hope you will enjoy working here.
We will reply as soon as possible.
However, business contracts can be difficult to understand.
section:
# PART 7
**Directions:** In this part you will read a selection of texts, such as magazine and newspaper articles, e-mails, and instant messages. Each text or set of texts is followed by several questions. Select the best answer for each question and mark the letter (A), (B), (C), or (D) on your answer sheet.
stimulus:
**Questions 147-148** refer to the following notice.
> **Cardinal Street Project—Update** > > Because of unusually wet and cold weather conditions, the road closure on Cardinal Street between Lee Drive and Petunia Lane will be extended until late spring. Construction will continue; however, final paving on Cardinal Street cannot occur until the weather conditions improve. Workers will begin the pavement work once construction of the bridge on Cardinal Street has been completed. If you have questions or comments, please contact Vy Nguyen in the city planning office at 615-555-0184.
stem: What is a purpose of the notice?
option_text: To report a change in weather patterns
To announce a new detour route
To explain a change in road construction plans
To highlight causes of road deterioration
stem: What is indicated about the Cardinal Street project?
option_text: It is being handled by Ms. Nguyen's company.
It does not involve street paving.
It cannot be completed in hot weather.
stimulus:
**Questions 149-150** refer to the following sign.
> **George's Orchard Farm** > **2232 Alpine Lane** > > Pick your own! Select from five varieties of juicy peaches in our orchard! Buckets provided at no cost. > > Stop by our market to purchase jams, pies, and sauces made from farm-fresh fruit. > We sell lunches to enjoy in our picnic area. > > Follow the signs ahead for designated parking areas. > > Wednesday to Friday, 12 noon to 5:00 P.M. > Saturday and Sunday, 10:00 A.M. to 6:00 P.M.
stem: What does George's Orchard Farm offer visitors?
What is indicated about the market?
option_text: It has a new parking area.
It sells products made with peaches.
It is open every day of the week.
stimulus:
**Questions 151-152** refer to the following e-mail.
| Field | Value | |---|---| | To: | Thandiwe Ngxongo | | From: | Customer Service | | Date: | 26 October | | Subject: | Performa lint roller (Model PL293) |
Dear Ms. Ngxongo,
I am sorry to inform you that the Performa lint roller you selected is on back order because of a shortage of its ultra-strong adhesive. We expect a new shipment within three to four weeks. However, if you prefer not to wait, Home Things will be glad to ship the Extreme2 lint roller to you at no extra charge. The Extreme2 model includes a perforated sticky tape for ease of use and comes with two refills. Like the Performa model, it has a strong adhesive that effectively removes hair, fuzz, lint, and dust from most fabrics.
You can request this similar product to be shipped to you in place of the one you ordered, as long as you make the change to your order before the original item ships. Thank you for your understanding.
Sincerely,
Glen McCraine Customer Service Specialist Home Things
stem: Why did Mr. McCraine write the e-mail?
option_text: To provide details about a return
stem: What is indicated about the Extreme2 lint roller?
option_text: It is less expensive than the Performa model.
It is more effective than the Performa model.
It has received high user ratings.
stimulus:
**Questions 153-154** refer to the following text-message chain.
> **Sen Chai (6:51 P.M.):** > We're on a dinner break from the negotiation meeting. Mr. Geraci did not accept our offer. > > **Mateo Michelacci (6:52 P.M.):** > That's frustrating. Do you think if we offered another €10,000 he would sell? You and I have agreed that this is the right facility: it's in a convenient location for customers, the access from the highway is perfect for incoming and outgoing deliveries, and potential tenants have already expressed interest. > > **Sen Chai (6:53 P.M.):** > I can't see that happening. But I'll try again. How high can I go? > > **Mateo Michelacci (6:54 P.M.):** > The bank approved us for another €20,000. Offer Mr. Geraci €10,000 more, plus we will fix any building code violations at our expense. Go up to the full €20,000 only if necessary. > > **Sen Chai (6:55 P.M.):** > I'll do my best.
stem: What is suggested about Mr. Geraci?
option_text: He will meet with Mr. Michelacci.
stem: At 6:53 P.M., what does Ms. Chai most likely mean when she writes, "I can't see that happening"?
option_text: She thinks that renters will be difficult to attract.
She believes that an offer will still be rejected.
She doubts that shipping will be easy to organize.
She thinks that clients will have trouble finding a location.
stimulus:
**Questions 155-157** refer to the following advertisement.
> **Blossom Sales Systems** > > Blossom Sales Systems (BSS) makes running your business a breeze with its sleek, intuitive point-of-sale devices. As a small business owner, you have many responsibilities and a reputation to build. BSS has all the tools needed to make transactions safe, seamless, and hassle-free. > > Enjoy the convenience of a system that has you covered wherever you conduct your business. Whether you sell in a store, over the phone, or online, BSS simplifies the transaction process while backing you with 24-7 technical expertise. Our innovative devices enable you to accept a variety of payment types—from magnetic-stripe and chip readers for credit cards to digital scanners for checks to popular mobile app payment services—ensuring that no potential sales are missed. > > Sign up for BSS today and enjoy a free ten-day trial. Registration takes just fifteen minutes to complete. Once approved, save $50 on the purchase of any BSS device that connects to your phone or tablet. Call 931-555-0148 to schedule a demonstration and see how BSS can make your sales bloom!
stem: The word "backing" in paragraph 2, line 3, is closest in meaning to
What payment method is NOT mentioned in the advertisement?
What is BSS offering as a sales promotion?
option_text: A free device for a tablet
stimulus:
**Questions 158-160** refer to the following article.
> **Bixby Electronics to Expand** > > Bixby Electronics announced yesterday that it will open a new manufacturing plant in the town of Clearwater. The company, which produces components for solar panels, has been looking for a location for a second plant for over a year. According to Bixby's CEO, Lian Huang, the company chose Clearwater because of its proximity to major highways and its skilled workforce. "We are excited to become part of the Clearwater community," Huang said at a press conference. "This expansion will allow us to increase production by 50 percent and meet the growing demand for renewable energy products." > > Construction on the new facility is scheduled to begin in March and is expected to be completed by the end of the year. The plant will create approximately 200 new jobs in the area. Local officials have welcomed the news, noting that the new jobs will provide a significant boost to the local economy. "This is a great day for Clearwater," said Mayor Patricia Dalton. "Bixby Electronics is a leader in the green energy sector, and we are proud that they have chosen our town for their expansion."
**158.** What is the article mainly about? (A) A company's plans for growth (B) A new solar panel design (C) A town's efforts to attract businesses (D) A CEO's career achievements
**159.** According to the article, why did Bixby Electronics select Clearwater? (A) The town offered tax incentives. (B) The town has a suitable location and workers. (C) The CEO previously lived in Clearwater. (D) The town has low energy costs.
**160.** What is indicated about the new plant? (A) It will replace an existing facility. (B) It will produce a new type of solar panel. (C) It will employ hundreds of workers. (D) It will be completed ahead of schedule.
**304**
**Questions 161-164** refer to the following online chat discussion.
> **Online Chat Discussion: Marketing Strategies** > > **Lena Park (9:10 A.M.):** Hi everyone. I've been reviewing our marketing strategies for the upcoming product launch. I think we should focus more on social media advertising. > > **David Chen (9:11 A.M.):** I agree, Lena. Social media is where our target audience spends most of their time. We could run targeted ads on platforms like Instagram and TikTok. > > **Lena Park (9:12 A.M.):** Exactly. I've been looking at some data, and our competitors are seeing great results with short video ads. > > **Maria Santos (9:13 A.M.):** That sounds promising, but we shouldn't forget about traditional media. Some of our older customers still prefer to get information through newspapers and magazines. > > **David Chen (9:14 A.M.):** That's a good point, Maria. Maybe we can allocate a portion of the budget to print ads while focusing most of our efforts on social media. > > **Lena Park (9:15 A.M.):** I think that's a reasonable compromise. We can propose a 70-30 split: 70 percent for social media and 30 percent for print. > > **Maria Santos (9:16 A.M.):** That works for me. I'll start drafting a proposal based on that allocation. > > **David Chen (9:17 A.M.):** Great. Let's meet again on Friday to review the draft before we present it to the management team.
**161.** What is the main topic of the discussion? (A) How to allocate a marketing budget (B) How to design a new product (C) How to hire new staff members (D) How to organize a company event
**162.** What type of advertising does Lena Park recommend? (A) Radio commercials (B) Billboard displays (C) Social media ads (D) Television spots
**163.** What concern does Maria Santos raise? (A) The budget is too limited. (B) Some customers prefer traditional media. (C) The launch date is too soon. (D) Competitors have better products.
**164.** What will happen on Friday? (A) A proposal will be reviewed. (B) A product will be launched. (C) A budget will be approved. (D) A new employee will be hired.
**GO ON TO THE NEXT PAGE**
TEST 10 **305**
**Questions 165-168** refer to the following e-mail and schedule.
> **E-mail** > > To: All Staff > From: Human Resources > Date: 5 May > Subject: Training Sessions > > Dear Colleagues, > > We are pleased to announce a series of professional development workshops to be held next month. The workshops will cover topics such as time management, effective communication, and leadership skills. Each session will be led by an expert trainer from the Professional Development Institute. > > Please review the attached schedule and sign up for the sessions that interest you. Space is limited, so we encourage you to register early. To sign up, please e-mail your selections to hr@company.com by 15 May. > > Best regards, > Human Resources > > **Attachment: Workshop Schedule** > > | Date | Time | Workshop Title | Location | > |---|---|---|---| > | 3 June | 9:00 A.M.–12:00 P.M. | Time Management Strategies | Room 201 | > | 3 June | 1:00 P.M.–4:00 P.M. | Effective Communication | Room 201 | > | 4 June | 9:00 A.M.–12:00 P.M. | Leadership Skills | Room 305 | > | 4 June | 1:00 P.M.–4:00 P.M. | Conflict Resolution | Room 305 | > | 5 June | 9:00 A.M.–12:00 P.M. | Project Management Basics | Room 201 | > | 5 June | 1:00 P.M.–4:00 P.M. | Team Building | Room 201 |
**165.** What is the purpose of the e-mail? (A) To announce a company holiday (B) To inform staff about training opportunities (C) To introduce a new human resources manager (D) To request feedback on recent workshops
**166.** What are staff members asked to do by 15 May? (A) Complete a survey (B) Pay a registration fee (C) Submit their workshop choices (D) Prepare a presentation
**167.** Which workshop is scheduled for the afternoon of 4 June? (A) Time Management Strategies (B) Effective Communication (C) Leadership Skills (D) Conflict Resolution
**168.** Where will the Project Management Basics workshop be held? (A) Room 201 (B) Room 305 (C) Room 401 (D) Room 502
**306**
**Questions 169-172** refer to the following article.
> **The Rise of Remote Work** > > The shift toward remote work has been one of the most significant changes in the business world over the past few years. According to a recent survey by Global Workplace Analytics, the number of people working remotely at least half of the time has increased by 140 percent since 2015. This trend has been driven by advances in technology, changing employee expectations, and the need for companies to reduce overhead costs. > > Many companies have reported positive results from allowing employees to work from home. Productivity has increased, and employee satisfaction has improved. However, remote work also presents challenges. Some managers find it difficult to supervise employees they do not see in person, and some workers struggle with feelings of isolation. > > To address these challenges, experts recommend that companies establish clear communication protocols and provide opportunities for remote workers to connect with their colleagues. Regular video meetings, virtual team-building activities, and occasional in-person gatherings can help maintain a sense of community and ensure that everyone stays aligned with company goals.
**169.** What is the article mainly about? (A) The history of office design (B) The growth and challenges of remote work (C) The benefits of traditional office environments (D) The future of technology companies
**170.** According to the article, what has contributed to the increase in remote work? (A) Government regulations (B) Advances in technology (C) Higher office rental costs (D) Decreased employee productivity
**171.** What challenge of remote work is mentioned in the article? (A) Higher salaries for remote workers (B) Difficulty in supervising employees (C) Increased office maintenance costs (D) Lack of access to technology
**172.** What do experts recommend to address the challenges of remote work? (A) Reducing employee salaries (B) Eliminating all in-person meetings (C) Establishing clear communication protocols (D) Requiring employees to work longer hours
**GO ON TO THE NEXT PAGE**
TEST 10 **307**
**Questions 173-176** refer to the following letter and form.
> **Letter** > > Dear Valued Customer, > > Thank you for your recent purchase of the UltraClean 3000 vacuum cleaner. We hope you are satisfied with your new appliance. To help you get the most out of your vacuum, we are enclosing a maintenance guide and a warranty registration form. > > Please complete the warranty registration form and return it to us within 30 days of your purchase. Registering your product will ensure that you receive prompt service in the event of any issues. As a registered owner, you will also be eligible for exclusive discounts on future purchases and accessories. > > If you have any questions, please do not hesitate to contact our customer service department at 1-800-555-0199. > > Sincerely, > > Home Appliance Co. > > **Warranty Registration Form** > > | Field | Information | > |---|---| > | Product Model | UltraClean 3000 | > | Date of Purchase | _______________ | > | Customer Name | _______________ | > | Address | _______________ | > | Phone Number | _______________ | > | E-mail Address | _______________ | > > Please mail this form to: Home Appliance Co., P.O. Box 452, Springfield, IL 62701
**173.** What is enclosed with the letter? (A) A product catalog (B) A discount coupon (C) A maintenance guide and a form (D) A customer satisfaction survey
**174.** How long does the customer have to return the registration form? (A) 14 days (B) 30 days (C) 60 days (D) 90 days
**175.** What is one benefit of registering the product? (A) A free accessory (B) An extended warranty (C) Discounts on future purchases (D) Free shipping on all orders
**176.** What information is NOT requested on the registration form? (A) Date of purchase (B) Customer's age (C) Phone number (D) E-mail address
**308**
**Questions 177-180** refer to the following e-mail and invoice.
> **E-mail** > > To: Accounts Payable > From: Maria Gonzalez > Date: 12 November > Subject: Invoice #4521 > > Dear Accounts Payable Department, > > I am writing to follow up on invoice #4521, which was sent to your office on 1 November. According to our records, payment for this invoice is now past due. The invoice was for the delivery of office supplies, including printer cartridges, paper, and filing cabinets, which were delivered to your main office on 25 October. > > I have attached a copy of the invoice for your reference. Please remit payment at your earliest convenience. If payment has already been made, please disregard this notice and accept our thanks. > > Should you have any questions or need to discuss payment arrangements, please contact me directly at 555-0198. > > Sincerely, > Maria Gonzalez > Billing Department, OfficeMax > > **Invoice** > > **OfficeMax** > 456 Commerce Drive > Invoice #4521 > Date: 25 October > > Bill To: GlobalTech Industries, 789 Innovation Way > > | Item | Quantity | Unit Price | Total | > |---|---|---|---| > | Printer Cartridges | 10 | $45.00 | $450.00 | > | Copy Paper (cases) | 20 | $35.00 | $700.00 | > | Filing Cabinets | 5 | $120.00 | $600.00 | > | **Subtotal** | | | **$1,750.00** | > | **Tax (8%)** | | | **$140.00** | > | **Total Due** | | | **$1,890.00** | > > Payment Terms: Net 15
**177.** Why did Ms. Gonzalez write the e-mail? (A) To confirm an order (B) To request payment for an invoice (C) To schedule a delivery (D) To complain about a product
**178.** What was the total amount due on the invoice? (A) $1,750.00 (B) $1,890.00 (C) $2,030.00 (D) $2,140.00
**179.** What does the invoice indicate about payment? (A) It is due within 15 days. (B) It must be made in cash. (C) It includes a late fee. (D) It has already been paid.
**180.** Which item on the invoice had the highest total cost? (A) Printer cartridges (B) Copy paper (C) Filing cabinets (D) Tax
**GO ON TO THE NEXT PAGE**
TEST 10 **309**
**Questions 181-185** refer to the following article and review.
> **Article: New Restaurant Opens Downtown** > > A new restaurant, The Green Table, opened last week on Main Street, and it is already generating buzz among local food enthusiasts. The restaurant, which specializes in farm-to-table cuisine, is owned by chef and restaurateur Olivia Barnes. Chef Barnes previously worked at several Michelin-starred restaurants in New York City before returning to her hometown to open her own establishment. > > The Green Table sources its ingredients from local farms and changes its menu seasonally. "I wanted to create a place where people could enjoy fresh, high-quality food in a relaxed atmosphere," Barnes said in an interview. The restaurant seats 80 people and features an open kitchen, allowing diners to watch the chefs at work. > > **Review: The Green Table** > > ★★★★☆ > > I visited The Green Table on its second night of operation, and I was impressed. The atmosphere is warm and inviting, with rustic wooden tables and soft lighting. The service was attentive without being intrusive. > > I started with the roasted beet salad, which was beautifully presented and full of flavor. For my main course, I chose the pan-seared salmon with seasonal vegetables. The salmon was cooked perfectly—crispy on the outside and tender on the inside. For dessert, I had the apple tart, which was delicious but slightly too sweet for my taste. > > Overall, The Green Table is a welcome addition to the downtown dining scene. I look forward to returning and trying more dishes from the menu.
**181.** What is The Green Table known for? (A) International cuisine (B) Farm-to-table cuisine (C) Fast food (D) Seafood specialties
**182.** What is indicated about Chef Olivia Barnes? (A) She is new to the restaurant industry. (B) She previously worked in New York City. (C) She owns several restaurants. (D) She is a food critic.
**183.** What feature of the restaurant is mentioned in the article? (A) A rooftop terrace (B) An open kitchen (C) A private dining room (D) A wine cellar
**184.** What did the reviewer say about the salmon? (A) It was undercooked. (B) It was too salty. (C) It was cooked perfectly. (D) It was not fresh.
**185.** What criticism did the reviewer have? (A) The service was slow. (B) The atmosphere was noisy. (C) The dessert was too sweet. (D) The portions were small.
**310**
**Questions 186-190** refer to the following e-mail and policy document.
> **E-mail** > > To: All Employees > From: Human Resources > Date: 1 September > Subject: Updated Vacation Policy > > Dear Employees, > > We are pleased to announce an update to our company's vacation policy. Based on feedback from staff surveys, we have made several changes designed to provide greater flexibility and work-life balance. The new policy will take effect on 1 January of next year. > > Key changes include an increase in the number of vacation days for employees with more than five years of service, the ability to carry over up to five unused vacation days to the following year, and a new system for requesting time off through the company's online portal. > > Please review the attached policy document carefully. If you have any questions, please contact the Human Resources department. > > **Attachment: Vacation Policy** > > **Vacation Policy (Effective 1 January)** > > **Eligibility:** All full-time employees are eligible for paid vacation time. > > **Vacation Days:** > - 1–5 years of service: 15 days per year > - 6–10 years of service: 20 days per year > - 11+ years of service: 25 days per year > > **Carryover:** Employees may carry over up to 5 unused vacation days to the following calendar year. Any unused days beyond this limit will be forfeited. > > **Requesting Time Off:** All vacation requests must be submitted through the online portal at least two weeks in advance. Requests will be approved based on staffing needs and on a first-come, first-served basis. > > **Holidays:** The company observes 10 paid holidays per year. These days are separate from vacation days.
**186.** What is the purpose of the e-mail? (A) To announce changes to the vacation policy (B) To remind employees to use their vacation days (C) To introduce a new human resources director (D) To request feedback on company policies
**187.** When will the new policy take effect? (A) 1 September (B) 1 October (C) 1 January (D) 1 March
**188.** How many vacation days will an employee with 8 years of service receive? (A) 15 days (B) 20 days (C) 25 days (D) 30 days
**189.** What is the maximum number of unused vacation days that can be carried over? (A) 3 days (B) 5 days (C) 10 days (D) 15 days
**190.** How far in advance must vacation requests be submitted? (A) One week (B) Two weeks (C) Three weeks (D) One month
**GO ON TO THE NEXT PAGE**
TEST 10 **311**
**Questions 191-195** refer to the following article and chart.
> **Global Smartphone Sales** > > Global smartphone sales reached 1.5 billion units last year, representing a 3 percent increase from the previous year. The market continues to be dominated by a handful of major manufacturers, with the top five companies accounting for over 70 percent of total sales. > > According to industry analysts, the growth was driven primarily by strong demand in emerging markets, where affordable smartphones are becoming increasingly accessible. In contrast, sales in mature markets such as North America and Western Europe have plateaued, as most consumers already own a smartphone and are upgrading less frequently. > > Looking ahead, analysts predict that sales will continue to grow modestly, with the introduction of 5G technology and foldable screens expected to drive future demand. However, supply chain disruptions and component shortages remain a concern for manufacturers. > > **Smartphone Sales by Region (Last Year)** > > | Region | Percentage of Global Sales | > |---|---| > | Asia-Pacific | 45% | > | North America | 15% | > | Europe | 18% | > | Latin America | 12% | > | Middle East & Africa | 10% |
**191.** What is the article mainly about? (A) The decline of the smartphone industry (B) Global smartphone sales and trends (C) A new smartphone manufacturer (D) The history of mobile phones
**192.** According to the article, what drove growth in smartphone sales? (A) Increased advertising (B) Lower prices in all markets (C) Strong demand in emerging markets (D) New government regulations
**193.** What is indicated about mature markets? (A) Sales are growing rapidly. (B) Sales have plateaued. (C) They have the highest percentage of sales. (D) They are unaffected by supply chain issues.
**194.** Which region accounted for the largest percentage of global smartphone sales? (A) North America (B) Europe (C) Asia-Pacific (D) Latin America
**195.** What concern for manufacturers is mentioned in the article? (A) Decreasing consumer interest (B) Supply chain disruptions (C) High advertising costs (D) Government regulations
**312**
**Questions 196-200** refer to the following e-mail, advertisement, and note.
> **E-mail** > > To: James Keller > From: Sarah Thompson > Date: 10 March > Subject: Venue for Company Retreat > > Dear James, > > I've been looking into venues for our company retreat in June. I found a place called Mountain View Lodge that looks promising. It's located about two hours from the city and offers meeting facilities, accommodations, and outdoor activities. I've attached their brochure. > > Could you take a look and let me know what you think? We need to make a decision by the end of the month. > > Thanks, > Sarah > > **Advertisement: Mountain View Lodge** > > **Mountain View Lodge** > Your Perfect Retreat Destination > > Located in the scenic Blue Ridge Mountains, Mountain View Lodge offers everything you need for a successful corporate retreat. Our facilities include: > - Three conference rooms with state-of-the-art audiovisual equipment > - 50 guest rooms with mountain views > - A full-service restaurant > - Outdoor activities including hiking trails, a ropes course, and a lake for kayaking > > **Special Offer:** Book your retreat by 31 March and receive a 15% discount on conference room rentals. > > For more information, call 1-800-555-0147 or visit www.mountainviewlodge.com. > > **Note** > > James— > > I called Mountain View Lodge this morning. They have availability for the weekends of 14–16 June and 21–23 June. The 14–16 June weekend would work better for our team. The total cost for 25 people, including meals and accommodations, would be approximately $12,500. With the 15% discount, it would be about $10,625. Let's discuss at tomorrow's meeting. > > —Sarah
**196.** What is the main purpose of Sarah's e-mail? (A) To confirm a reservation (B) To suggest a venue for a retreat (C) To request time off (D) To cancel a meeting
**197.** What is NOT listed as a feature of Mountain View Lodge? (A) Conference rooms (B) A swimming pool (C) Hiking trails (D) A restaurant
**198.** What special offer is mentioned in the advertisement? (A) A free night's stay (B) A discount on conference room rentals (C) Free meals for all guests (D) Complimentary transportation
**199.** Which weekend does Sarah recommend? (A) 7–9 June (B) 14–16 June (C) 21–23 June (D) 28–30 June
**200.** What would be the approximate cost with the discount? (A) $12,500 (B) $11,250 (C) $10,625 (D) $9,500
**Stop.** This is the end of the Reading test.
<|END|> **Questions 155-157** refer to the following advertisement.
> **Blossom Sales Systems** > > Blossom Sales Systems (BSS) makes running your business a breeze with its sleek, intuitive point-of-sale devices. As a small business owner, you have many responsibilities and a reputation to build. BSS has all the tools needed to make transactions safe, seamless, and hassle-free. > > Enjoy the convenience of a system that has you covered wherever you conduct your business. Whether you sell in a store, over the phone, or online, BSS simplifies the transaction process while backing you with 24-7 technical expertise. Our innovative devices enable you to accept a variety of payment types—from magnetic-stripe and chip readers for credit cards to digital scanners for checks to popular mobile app payment services—ensuring that no potential sales are missed. > > Sign up for BSS today and enjoy a free ten-day trial. Registration takes just fifteen minutes to complete. Once approved, save $50 on the purchase of any BSS device that connects to your phone or tablet. Call 931-555-0148 to schedule a demonstration and see how BSS can make your sales bloom!
> **\*E-mail\*** > > | | | > |---|---| > | To: | cbrandt@prebleevents.com | > | From: | chsiao@yanvillefestival.com | > | Date: | May 15 | > | Subject: | Festival support | > > Dear Mr. Brandt, > > Thank you for contacting us regarding the Yanville Festival. — [1] —. We have already contracted with Breemer's Staging to provide lighting equipment for our main stages. > > — [2] —. Do you provide catering services? — [3] —. We do not have anyone yet to manage the food for our actors and stage crews. Would you be interested in this work? Please let me know. — [4] —. > > Best regards, > > Cecily IIsiao > Vice-Chair, Yanville Festival
**158.** What is one reason that Ms. Hsiao wrote the e-mail? - (A) To confirm an order - (B) To request a price - (C) To provide a report - (D) To make an offer
**159.** What most likely is the focus of the Yanville Festival? - (A) Music - (B) Books - (C) Theater - (D) Fashion
**160.** In which of the positions marked [1], [2], [3], and [4] does the following sentence best belong?
> "However, we have heard good reports about your company, Preble Events, from our partners."
- (A) [1] - (B) [2] - (C) [3] - (D) [4]
**Questions 161-163** refer to the following advertisement.
> **Kramer's Emporium** > 2323 Raleigh Street • Houston, TX 77021 > > After 25 years in business, our store is closing. Everything must go! > > From refrigerators to washing machines, we have hundreds of brand-new appliances for your home or office. Most items have been marked down by 30 percent. Delivery is available within 60 miles of our store for a flat fee of $50.00. We are open daily from 9:00 A.M. to 7:00 P.M. > > Don't wait! Merchandise is selling fast!
> | | | > |---|---| > | To: | Bruce Holt | > | From: | Shamonica Walker | > | Date: | April 9 | > | Subject: | Immediate and confidential | > > Dear Bruce, > > Beginning with the new fiscal year, a change in retirement benefits will be applied for all full-time employees here in Blanen Industries. They will no longer be given their matching retirement funds in the form of cash. Instead, their retirement funds will be provided in the form of company stock to be deposited in their retirement portfolios. > > Our current retirement management firm, Proxave Futures, has been informed of this change in retirement benefits. The firm will manage the transition and will continue to provide customer support to Blanen Industries employees. Questions should be directed to Pierre Dehaene at Proxave Futures rather than our human resources department. > > Please work with Yua Suzuki, head of human resources, to provide her with the information she should include in her all-staff communication about this important change in employee benefits. Our president notified me that he would like to receive all reports related to this transition. Also, please continue to send me daily financial reports. > > Best, > > Shamonica Walker, CFO > Blanen Industries
> **Monica Zia (8:27 A.M.)** Hi, Yuji and Sung-ho. I wonder if one of you could help me later today. Dejani Jones was going to help, but she's going to be out of the office today. > > **Yuji Saito (8:28 A.M.)** That's too bad. Did she say why? > > **Monica Zia (8:28 A.M.)** She said she had a slight cold but that she should be able to work from home on the data analyses for her clients. > > **Sung-ho Suh (8:29 A.M.)** So, she won't be coming in at all today? > > **Monica Zia (8:31 A.M.)** No. And Dejani and I were going to go to Willi's Market to buy food and beverages for tomorrow's office picnic. You remember, right? Saturday at noon at City Park. Anyhow, Dejani has a car and was going to drive us to the store and use her credit card to purchase the supplies. I know that both of you drive to work, so I thought one of you could help. > > **Sung-ho Suh (8:32 A.M.)** When were you thinking of going? > > **Yuji Saito (8:32 A.M.)** Sure. I can help, and we can use my credit card. > > **Monica Zia (8:33 A.M.)** Around two. > > **Yuji Saito (8:34 A.M.)** No problem. Just stop by my cubicle, and we can take my car to go to the store. > > **Sung-ho Suh (8:35 A.M.)** That's great, Yuji. I have several meetings this afternoon, so I wouldn't be able to get away. > > **Monica Zia (8:35 A.M.)** Thanks, Yuji. I'll see you this afternoon.
> | | | > |---|---| > | To: | Dorota Kucharski | > | From: | Luciano Moretti | > | Date: | 8 August | > | Subject: | Research results | > > Ms. Kucharski, > > The following is a summary of what I have learned in my research about where to hold our next annual technology conference in June of next year. > > In Rome, the best option I have found is Hotel al Ponte. The total cost for conference events there would be €31,500. — [1] —. Individual hotel rooms would cost €80 per night for participants. In the event that attendance is high, we could also house participants in the nearby Hotel Milvio at €120 per night. — [2] —. > > I also looked into hotels in Genoa and Florence, but I could not find any that would be much cheaper in overall conference costs. — [3] —. More importantly, I am concerned that the greater cost of flights to those smaller cities could discourage some attendees. Rome, on the other hand, has affordable direct flights from most European cities. — [4] —. It will also be more affordable for our colleagues coming from Buenos Aires. > > If you would like to pursue the Hotel al Ponte option, I will provide a detailed write-up of the anticipated costs and of the amenities offered by the hotel. > > Best, > > Luciano Moretti > Secretary, International Technological Society
> https://www.centraluniversity.edu/nutrition/newsletter > > | Home | Customer Service | Subscribe | Account | Log In | > > **Central University Health Newsletter—Your Guide to Nutrition and Well-Being** > > Tips on healthful eating are available almost everywhere—from TV shows to online cooking forums. It's often difficult to know whether the advice you encounter is based on scientific evidence. That's why the *Central University Health Newsletter* is indispensable. Each month, it delivers informative, easy-to-understand articles that summarize findings by researchers at the Central University School of Nutrition. For example, last April's issue evaluated nutritional supplements that are sold in supermarkets. The back page features our Ask an Expert column, in which the director of the School of Nutrition answers readers' questions. > > A one-year subscription is $20 and includes online access to all previous issues. We also offer a 10 percent discount on your first subscription renewal. What's more, subscribers receive our free weekly "Healthy Living" e-mail update, which includes special reports plus links to videos and podcasts. > > To get started, simply go to our Subscribe page and follow the instructions.
> https://www.centraluniversity.edu/nutrition/newsletter/reviews > > **Publication:** *Central University Health Newsletter* > ★★★★★ > **Reviewed by:** Ari Kipp > **Date posted:** August 4 > > This is my second year subscribing to the *Central University Health Newsletter*, which I read cover to cover. I'm particularly impressed with the Ask an Expert column, and I always learn something new. This month, for instance, Dr. Stella Booth gives a surprising answer to the question of which pasta is most nutritious. I didn't know that bean-based pasta even existed, much less how much fiber it contains. I tried it and will never go back to regular pasta. My sole complaint is that the newsletter is only twelve pages long!
310
> **Brit-Revision Editing Services Invoice** > > **Freelance Editor:** Lisa Yamashita > **Invoice Date:** 30 September > **Address:** 178 Upsala Road > LONDON > E16 1DJ > **Contact:** (020) 7946 0612 > lisa.yamashita@bluesun.co.uk > Stashcash ID: Lisa.Yamashita8 > > | Client Name | Project Description | Date Completed | Time | > |---|---|---|---| > | Eddie Kent | University thesis editing | 5 September | 2 hours | > | Ben Gallagher | Grant proposal editing | 9 September | 10 hours | > | Lydia Quinn of Hyden Interiors | Marketing copy editing | 17 September | 8.5 hours | > | Winona Rogers | Journal article editing | 22 September | 2 hours | > | Tony Withers | CV and cover letter editing | 28 September | 2.5 hours |
> | | | > |---|---| > | **To:** | Lisa Yamashita | > | **From:** | Claudio Aguilar | > | **Date:** | 1 October | > | **Subject:** | Your recent invoice | > > Dear Lisa, > > Thank you for sending your invoice for services rendered in the month of September. I want to commend you on your superb start as one of our independent freelance editors. Our clients have nothing but positive things to say about your work. As you know, we do not dictate how you structure your workday; instead, Brit-Revision (BRV) guidelines require simply that deadlines be met and that work quality be maintained. You have done both admirably. > > Also, we will pay you for an additional 30 minutes of work that you did for Mr. Kent. He reported that you did consulting work for him over the phone on a short oral presentation that he created. As for the work that you did for our long-standing corporate client Lydia Quinn, you will see on your receipt that we have paid you at a higher hourly rate. > > I have initiated the transfer of £750 into your Stashcash account. The funds should appear in your account by tomorrow morning. > > Sincerely, > > Claudio Aguilar > Accounts Payable, Brit-Revision Editing Services
312
**Questions 186-190** refer to the following e-mails and instructions.
> | | | > |---|---| > | **To:** | New Employees | > | **From:** | Hemi Amos | > | **Date:** | June 8 | > | **Subject:** | Welcome to Motman Motors | > | **Attachment:** | 📎 Information | > > Dear New Employees, > > Welcome! We are thrilled that all of you are joining Motman Motors. > > New-employee orientation for all is on June 10 from 9:00 A.M. to 12:00 noon. Please report to the proper room in the Pimzler Building on our campus as indicated below. I will come around to each room to meet the new employees briefly. > > - Marketing and Customer Service: both groups report to room 320. > - Engineering: report to room 215. > - Technology Services: report to room 158. > > I can confirm that the employee paperwork has been finalized for most of you, so at this point we just need to ensure that the Motman Motors portal account is set up using the employee identification number recently sent to you and that your bank account information is entered there. Please follow the attached instructions to complete this final step. It should not take any more than fifteen minutes, and I ask that you do this by June 17. Don't hesitate to reach out if you have any questions. > > Welcome aboard! > > Hemi Amos > Human Resources, Motman Motors
> Please follow these steps to create and activate your new Motman Motors portal account. Go to https://motmanmotors.com/portal/activation to begin. > > 1. Go to the "New User Registration" page and enter your employee ID number. > 2. Your name and address will appear. Confirm that this information is correct. > 3. A new screen titled "Bank Account" will appear. Fill in your bank account information. > 4. Finally, you will be asked to select two security questions and provide the answers. > > Once you have created your account, your account will become active, the payroll department will be alerted, and your payment schedule will be set. Please e-mail Mr. Hemi Amos at hamos@motmanmotors.com if you have any difficulties or questions.
314
| | | |---|---| | **To:** | Hemi Amos | | **From:** | Karuna Dimaano | | **Date:** | June 9 | | **Subject:** | Trouble with Motman Motors portal |
Dear Mr. Amos,
Thank you for the kind welcome e-mail yesterday.
I am trying to set up my Motman Motors portal account, but I seem to be having trouble with my employee ID number. Each time I put the information into the system, I get an error message. I might not have the right number. Can you please advise me on how to address this?
I also have another question. Will the orientation include any information for newcomers to San Antonio? I am excited to start exploring everything here that this great city offers.
I look forward to meeting you tomorrow in room 215 in the Pimzler Building.
Thank you,
Karuna Dimaano
**Questions 191-195** refer to the following e-mail, invoice, and memo.
| | | |---|---| | **To:** | Felix Herman | | **From:** | Sapna Mathai | | **Date:** | September 17 | | **Subject:** | Notes on rough cut of video | | **Attachment:** | Information |
Dear Mr. Herman,
Thank you for sending the rough version of the informational video for our new Fluorolook Imager. I think that the video looks great so far. It clearly demonstrates why the Fluorolook is the best medical imaging device available. I have just a few notes before you proceed to create the final version.
- At 20 seconds: Could you insert a short timeline of the history of The Hospital Equipment Company? I have attached the relevant information to this e-mail. - At 1 minute: I had a hard time seeing the details of the control panel in this scene. Could you please replace it with a close-up shot? - At 2 minutes: The sequence in which the medical technician positions the patient and adjusts the machine moves too quickly. Can this be slowed down?
I look forward to seeing the final version on September 23. As always, thank you and your colleagues at Video Genie Productions for the excellent work.
Sincerely,
Ms. Sapna Mathai, Project Manager The Hospital Equipment Company
### The Hospital Equipment Company
Invoice 9984 **Billing date:** October 25 **Installation date:** October 26
Bill to: All-City Hospital 3 Shoreline Road Clear Lake, Minnesota 55319
| Quantity | Item Number | Description | Unit Price | Total | |----------|-------------|-------------|------------|-------| | 2 | 62630 | Fluorolook Imager | $242,300 | $484,600 | | | | **Delivery and Installation** | | $2,350 | | | | **Total** | | **$486,950** |
316
## MEMO
**To:** All The Hospital Equipment Company Employees **From:** Marcia Oliver, Vice President of Sales **Date:** October 27 **Re:** Good work!
Congratulations on the successful launch of the Fluorolook Imager! The Hospital Equipment Company has made its first sale—to a hospital in Clear Lake. And MDP Delivery has informed us that the two devices have been delivered.
I would especially like to commend Ms. Mathai. She was instrumental in getting us to this point. The administrator of the hospital that made the purchase, Mr. Fabrice Lamontagne, credited his decision to the informational video. He remarked that the video effectively demonstrated why the Fluorolook Imager was the right device for his medical institution.
**Questions 196-200** refer to the following packing slip and e-mails.
### Packing Slip
| | | |---|---| | **From:** Reina Olive Oil Company | **To:** Best Products Supermarket | | Calle Tablas 820 | 241 Yeonhae-ro, Jung-gu | | 18002 Granada, Spain | Incheon 22382, Republic of Korea |
**Order Date:** 28 November **Order Number:** SK6224
| Item Number | Description | Quantity | |-------------|-------------|----------| | EVO160 | 750-millilitre extra-virgin olive oil standard size glass cruet with handle | 15 cases | | EVO161 | 1-litre extra-virgin olive oil family size glass bottle | 20 cases | | EVO162 | 1.5-litre extra-virgin olive oil chef size glass bottle | 10 cases | | EVO163 | 3-litre extra-virgin olive oil bulk size Reina logo tin | 5 cases | | EVO001 | 50-millilitre extra-virgin olive oil December sample size | 2 cases |
Celebrate the season with us! Place your next order by 31 January to receive 10 percent off.
### *E-mail*
| | | |---|---| | **To:** | Pablo Nadal | | **From:** | Min Hae Seong | | **Date:** | 12 December | | **Subject:** | Order number SK6224 |
Dear Mr. Nadal:
We appreciate your fulfilling our recent order of extra-virgin olive oil so quickly. Unfortunately, there is a small issue with the shipment, and we need your assistance. While nothing was damaged or missing, the 3-litre bulk size containers were delivered in error.
We would like to arrange to send these products back. Please inform us how to proceed.
Thank you,
Min Hae Seong Receiving and Distribution, Best Products Supermarket
318
| | | |---|---| | **To:** | Min Hae Seong | | **From:** | Pablo Nadal | | **Date:** | 14 December | | **Subject:** | RE: Order number SK6224 |
Dear Ms. Seong,
I apologise for the error in order number SK6224. We have determined that your order was accidentally combined with another in our electronic database. Unfortunately, we are unable to restock any items that have left our warehouse. Therefore, you may keep the additional olive oil and sell it in your stores. Also, you will receive a refund for the extra charge within the next five to ten business days because of our mistake.
We are currently celebrating peak olive season by offering 10 percent off of your next order. Also, during our Olive Harvest Festival in December, every shipment includes samples of our company's classic olive oil that we encourage you to give to your shoppers with our compliments.
We hope to continue providing Best Products Supermarket with the finest olive oils.
Sincerely yours,
Pablo Nadal Export Division Manager, Reina Olive Oil Company
